title: Problem Solving: Model Multiplication
essential_question: How can you use the strategy draw a picture to solve equal group problems?
strategies: Draw a picture to show equal groups
Use bar models to represent multiplication
Write a multiplication sentence to solve

items.question: There are 5 shelves with 6 video games each. How many games in all?
Draw a picture: 3 groups of 7. How many in all?
Ken sees 2 rows of flute players with 6 in each row, plus 8 trombone players. How many flute or trombone players?
Sara buys 4 packs of stickers. Each pack has 8 stickers. How many stickers?
A toy box has 3 layers. Each layer has 9 toys. Model with multiplication. How many toys?

items.title: Drawing a Picture to Multiply
items.content: Draw circles for groups and dots inside for items. 4 groups of 3: draw 4 circles, put 3 dots in each. Count all dots: 12. So 4 × 3 = 12.

items.title: Bar Models for Multiplication
items.content: A bar model shows equal parts. Draw a long bar split into equal sections. Each section = size of group. Number of sections = number of groups. Total = all sections combined.

items.title: Two-Step Problems
items.content: Some problems need two steps: first multiply, then add or subtract. Example: 3 bags of 5 apples, then eat 2. Step 1: 3×5=15. Step 2: 15−2=13.

items.question: Model: 6 groups of 3. How many?
hints: Think about what model multiplication means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.
